compressed_prompt: write song lyrics on socio-economic issue, all lowercase, no commas
response:
**"brick by brick"**

i wake up to the sound of sirens
another factory closed its doors
the for sale sign’s been up for months now
but nobody’s buying anymore

mama’s working double shifts
daddy’s got no job to keep
the bank just called again
said we’re drowning in the deep

brick by brick they built the walls
brick by brick they shut us out
brick by brick we’re breaking down
while they count their stacks and shout

the school’s got no books no heat
the clinic’s got no meds
the preacher says it’s god’s will
but i don’t see him in the bread

the rich man’s got a golden gate
the poor man’s got a rusted key
they tell us pull ourselves up
but the ladder’s got no rungs for me

brick by brick they built the walls
brick by brick they shut us out
brick by brick we’re breaking down
while they count their stacks and shout

the news says times are getting better
but my neighbor’s got no food
the politicians shake our hands
then they vote to cut our paychecks too

we march with signs and tired feet
they call us lazy call us loud
but who’s the one with empty plates
and who’s the one with pockets proud?

brick by brick they built the walls
brick by brick they shut us out
brick by brick we’re breaking down
while they count their stacks and shout

so here’s to the ones who work all day
here’s to the ones who can’t get paid
here’s to the ones they left behind
brick by brick we’ll tear it down this time
